description: Accessing a field that a struct type does not declare fires E-TYPE-004 (§14.3). `p` is a local typed `Point` (fields x, y); `p.bogus` names no declared field, so the type system reports E-TYPE-004 naming the offending field and the available field set. Incidental W-PROGRAM-SPA-INFERRED is ignored by superset matching.
rationale: §14.3 struct field access is nominal: a member access on a struct-typed binding must name a declared field, otherwise the compiler SHALL error (E-TYPE-004). The check runs at the type-system stage over the local's resolved struct type.
